REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF EXPRESS AND IMPLIED CONTRACT, THE COURT NOTES THAT THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NO LONGER ALLEGED IN THE ALTERNATIVE (AS THEY HAVE BEEN ALLEGED IN THE PRIOR ITERATION OF THE COMPLAINT). THIS CHANGE IN THE ALLEGATIONS RENDERS THE COMPLAINT UNCERTAIN AS TO WHICH CONTRACTUAL TERMS ARE EXPRESS AND WHICH ARE IMPLIED. ALLEGATIONS IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT LIST IDENTICAL TERMS IN PARAGRAPH 11 AND PARAGRAPH 30. HOWEVER THERE CANNOT BE A VALID, EXPRESS CONTRACT AND AN IMPLIED CONTRACT, EACH EMBRACING THE SAME SUBJECT MATTER, EXISTING AT THE SAME TIME. THE ONLY CONTRACTUAL TERM THAT IS LISTED SOLELY UNDER THE "IMPLIED CONTACT" IS CONTAINED IN PARAGRAPH 35 REGARDING PARTIES AGREEMENT TO SHARE THEIR WEALTH AND ACCUMULATION OF ASSETS DURING THEIR RELATIONSHIP IN EQUAL SHARES. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT SHE PERFORMED UNDER THIS TERM OR HER PERFORMANCE WAS FRUSTRATED. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE EXPRESS AND IMPLIED CONTRACTS IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO ALLEGE THE FORMATION AND THE TERMS OF (1) THE ORAL CONTRACT AND (2) THE FORMATION AND THE TERMS OF THE IMPLIED CONTRACT. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF APPEARS TO SUGGEST THAT SHE DID NOT BELIEVE THAT THE CONTRACTS (REGARDING SHARING THE ASSETS ACCUMULATED DURING THEIR RELATIONSHIP) WERE BINDING ENFORCEABLE AGREEMENTS UNTIL SHE CONSULTED HER ATTORNEYS SOMETIME AFTER DEFENDANT'S PURPORTED BREACH IN DECEMBER 2021. THIS ASSERTION (WHILE NOT YET PLED IN ANY ITERATION OF THE COMPLAINT, BUT ARGUED IN OPPOSITION) SUGGESTS THAT AT THE TIME THE CONTRACT(S) WERE FORMED (2013-2014) THERE COULD NOT HAVE BEEN MUTUAL ASSENT TO THE TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF SHALL ALLEGE FACTS REGARDING MUTUAL ASSENT TO THE FORMATION OF THE CONTRACT(S). (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF'S FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS STRICKEN. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER TO RECONCILE THE INCONSISTENT POSITIONS TAKEN IN 2019 FILING AND IN THIS COMPLAINT REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS TO THE REAL PROPERTY AND LLCS. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO OBTAIN LEAVE OF COURT TO ALLEGE A NEW CAUSE OF ACTION (ALBEIT REFERENCES TO CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST AS A REMEDY EXISTED IN THE PRIOR VERSION OF THE COMPLAINT AS A REMEDY). PLAINTIFF FURTHER FAILS TO CITE ANY CASES FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT A CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST REMEDY IS AVAILABLE UNDER BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS NOT PRECLUDED FROM SEEKING CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST AS A REMEDY IF CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT SUPPORT SUCH RELIEF. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO ANY REFERENCES TO THE REAL PROPERTY ASSERTED IN CONJUNCTION WITH THE CLAIM TO INTEREST OR VALUE OF THE PROPERTY. (THIS ISSUE MAY BE MOOT IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF'S REPRESENTATION THAT SHE WILL NOT PROCEED WITH ANY OF HER PROPERTY CLAIMS IN THIS JURISDICTION). MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPH 39 WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO EXPLAIN THE CHANGE FROM "5 YEARS" TO "7 YEARS."MOTION IS DENIED AT TO PARAGRAPHS 11, 30 AND 35. WHILE THESE ALLEGATIONS ARE DIFFERENT FROM THE PRIOR VERSION OF THE COMPLAINT, THEY ARE MORE EXPANSIVE, AND NOT ENTIRELY INCONSISTENT TO TRIGGER THE SHAM PLEADINGS DOCTRINE. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS TO SUPPORT MALICE, OPPRESSION OR FRAUD. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-04-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. The Third Amended Complaint alleges the terms of the agreement between the parties (paragraphs 11, 30, 23 and 24). Those are the terms to be adjudicated in this action. Plaintiff stated the facts regarding the purported date of formation of the contract and the date of the breach. Those facts will be taken as true, and the Court will disregard the incorrect math/arithmetic. The reason for Plaintiff's contradictory statements in the divorce case in this action is an issue of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer. Reading the Complaint as a whole, the existence and breach of fiduciary duty is sufficiently stated. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. The Court Notes that both sides chose to submit oversized briefs not heeding the Court's instructions on the prior demurrer. This is the last warning that any failure to comply with CRC or SFLR in the future will result in motions being denied or oppositions being stricken.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT JAMES CUNNINGHAM, JAMES CUNNINGHAM DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT
is SUSTAINED. As to the causes of action for breach of express and implied contract, the Court notes that these causes of action are no longer alleged in the alternative (as they have been alleged in the prior iteration of the complaint). This change in the allegations renders the complaint uncertain as to which contractual terms are express and which are implied. Allegations in the Second Amended Complaint list identical terms in paragraph 11 and paragraph 30. However there cannot be a valid, express contract and an implied contract, each embracing the same subject matter, existing at the same time. The only contractual term that is listed solely under the "implied contact" is contained in paragraph 35 regarding parties agreement to share their wealth and accumulation of assets during their relationship in equal shares. Plaintiff does not allege that she performed under this term or her performance was frustrated. Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to allege express and implied contracts in the alternative, to allege the formation and the terms of (1) the oral contract and (2) the formation and the terms of the implied contract. The Court further notes that in opposition to the Motion to Strike Plaintiff appears to suggest that she did not believe that the contracts (regarding sharing the assets accumulated during their relationship) were binding enforceable agreements until she consulted her attorneys sometime after defendant's purported breach in December 2021. This assertion (while not yet pled in any iteration of the complaint, but argued in opposition) suggests that at the time the contract(s) were formed (2013-2014) there could not have been mutual assent to the terms of the agreement. Therefore, Plaintiff shall allege facts regarding mutual assent to the formation of the contract(s).
Demurrer to the cause of action for constructive trust is moot. See ruling on the Motion to Strike.
Parties' requests for judicial notice are granted.
Additionally, all declarations (except for meet and confer declaration) submitted in conjunction with the briefs on this action are stricken. There is no authority cited for the proposition that declarations may be considered on a demurrer.
Finally, the last two pages of Plaintiff's Points and Authorities in support of the opposition are stricken. Plaintiff did not seek and did not receive a permission to file an oversized brief and therefore the brief is limited to 15 pages. In the future, if oversized briefs are submitted, the Court will strike the entire brief.
Plaintiff shall have 10 days to file an amended complaint.
It appears that Plaintiff at this time wishes to remove any claims (ownership, value, etc) to the real property. If the amended complaint removes any claims associated with real property, all future motions shall be calendared in department 302.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT JAMES CUNNINGHAM MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff's fourth amended complaint is stricken. Plaintiff failed to comply with the Court's prior order to reconcile the inconsistent positions taken in 2019 filing and in this complaint regarding Plaintiff's claims to the real property and LLCs. Plaintiff failed to obtain leave of Court to allege a new cause of action (albeit references to constructive trust as a remedy existed in the prior version of the complaint as a remedy). Plaintiff further fails to cite any cases for the proposition that a constructive trust remedy is available under breach of contract cause of action. Plaintiff is not precluded from seeking constructive trust as a remedy if causes of action in the amended complaint support such relief.
Motion is granted as to any references to the real property asserted in conjunction with the claim to interest or value of the property. (This issue may be moot in light of Plaintiff's representation that she will not proceed with any of her property claims in this jurisdiction).
Motion is granted as to paragraph 39 with leave to amend to explain the change from "5 years" to "7 years."
Motion is denied at to paragraphs 11, 30 and 35. While these allegations are different from the prior version of the complaint, they are more expansive, and not entirely inconsistent to trigger the sham pleadings doctrine.
Motion is granted as to the request for punitive damages with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions to support malice, oppression or fraud.
Plaintiff shall have 10 days to file an amended complaint.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jeanine Zalduendo; Zweiback, Fiset & Zalduendo LLP; 213-266-5170; appearing as counsel for defendant James J. Cunningham.
Alexander Ramon; Hoge Fenton Jones & Appel, Inc.; 408-287-9501; appearing as counsel for plaintiff Heather Gregg.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JAMES CUNNINGHAM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Defendant's request for judicial notice is GRANTED. The Court will take notice of the filings, they legal effect and existence statements/omissions contained therein. Judicial notice is not taken of veracity of any statements contained therein, unless they relevant finding of law or fact contained in a final court order order/judgment.
As to the cause of action for quiet title and related relief contained in the cause of action for declaratory relief, demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile her claims to the property with the lack of such claims in prior judicial proceedings. Plaintiff must also determine weather Plaintiff intends to proceed with this claim in personam in this jurisdiction or in rem in Massachusetts. This court will not proceed with this action for a personal judgment which will later have to be enforced in Massachusetts if Plaintiff is at the same time proceeding in Massachusetts. If Plaintiff decides to maintain an action in Massachusetts, Plaintiff shall not re-allege these claims in the amended complaint. If Plaintiff dismisses the action in Massachusetts, Plaintiff shall file an amended complaint consistent with this ruling.

As to the remainder of the cause of action for declaratory relief Plaintiff shall specify the assets referenced in this cause of action but not listed. If this cause of action is limited to the two assets specifically listed therein, amended complaint shall so allege. Furthermore, Plaintiff shall also allege facts supporting the claim to interest in Twyford Abbey, LLC July 2016 investment (an asset) in light of the judicially noticeable documents.
As to the causes of action for promissory estopel, express and implied contract, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Complaint sufficiently asserts alternative theories for recovery. Complaint also sufficiently asserts facts to support some consideration by Plaintiff. However, Complaint does not sufficiently state the terms of the contract/promises to be enforced. Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to allege the formation of the contract(s) and the exact terms of the contract(s).
Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Tanya Rogers, CSR #10178, elitecourtreporter@gmail.com, 805-561-9050 (D501)
